items.title: Risky Business #841 -- Microsoft gets owned and 0day'd
items.summary: On this week’s show special guest co-host Chris Wade, the founder of Corellium turned Cellebrite CTO, joins Patrick Gray and James Wilson to discuss the week’s cybersecurity news. They cover: Microsoft has repos owned, GitHub tokens popped, and a new 0day dropped on them Meanwhile, researchers are c

items.title: KnowBe4 launches game to train staff on vishing scams
items.summary: Phone-based fraud is forcing employers to train staff more aggressively as vishing losses rise and call scams spread across Australia.

items.title: Enterprises shift AI workloads towards private cloud
items.summary: Rising costs, security worries and data sovereignty are pushing more firms to run production AI inferencing in private cloud, a Broadcom survey shows.

items.title: Gallagher to host Western Australia Security Forum in Perth
items.summary: Security operators in Western Australia will get updates on protecting critical assets and integrating systems at the Perth forum.

items.title: Elastic adds Kubernetes incident workflow for AI tools
items.summary: Outages in Kubernetes clusters can now be triaged automatically inside AI tools, cutting the time on-call engineers spend hunting root causes.

items.title: Cyber criminals target World Cup staff & suppliers
items.summary: Attackers are using fake World Cup sites and messaging apps to steal credentials, with some scams now aimed at event suppliers and staff.

items.title: Australian football fans trust fake Wi-Fi networks
items.summary: Most Australian fans would still join venue-named hotspots, leaving match-day travellers exposed to phishing, fake streams and account theft.

items.title: How Australians can spot FIFA World Cup scams
items.summary: Australians planning to attend the FIFA World Cup in North America next year are being warned to watch for ticketing and related scams that typically surge around major sporting events, [...]

items.title: Datadog launches 100 AI tools for operations & security
items.summary: The rollout aims to help customers tame rising AI-driven complexity as Datadog adds autonomous monitoring, security and agent oversight tools.

items.title: Microsoft, Australian Government sign MOU on cloud, cybersecurity and AI cooperation
items.summary: Microsoft and the Australian Government have signed a memorandum of understanding (MOU) intended to strengthen national digital resilience, with a focus on secure cloud infrastructure, cybersecurity, artificial intelligence and critical [...]

items.title: A Record-Breaking Patch Tuesday for June 2026
items.summary: Microsoft today released software updates to plug nearly 200 security holes across its Windows operating systems and supported software, a record number of fixes for the company's monthly Patch Tuesday cycle. Nearly three dozen of those bugs earned Microsoft's most dire "critical" rating, and exploi

items.title: Blame AI: Patch Tuesday Hits Record 206 CVEs
items.summary: Voluminous patch updates could soon be the norm, as artificial intelligence accelerates the speed and scale of vulnerability discovery.

items.title: ServiceNow discloses security incident exposing customer data - BleepingComputer
items.summary: ServiceNow discloses security incident exposing customer data BleepingComputer

items.title: Miasma Supply Chain Worm Burrows Into 73 Microsoft Repositories
items.summary: The attacks stemmed from a GitHub account that was also compromised in a previous Miasmi attack on Microsoft last month.

items.title: Russian Attackers Weaponize WinRAR Flaw Against Ukrainian Orgs
items.summary: Two separate campaigns target CVE-2025-8088, fixed last July, to conduct data theft and cyberespionage against military and government targets in Ukraine.

items.title: Silent Ransom Group Hits US Law Firms in Escalating Extortion Attacks
items.summary: The financially motivated group is combining vishing, IT impersonation, and in-person office intrusions to steal data and extort victims.

items.title: Check Point VPN Flaw Exploited Since Early May
items.summary: A newly discovered, critical zero-day vulnerability is under attack; a Qilin ransomware affiliate has been blamed for at least one incident.

items.title: Iran Signed a Ceasefire — Its Hackers Didn't
items.summary: An extension of the Geneva Conventions could impose restrictions on cyberwarfare under ceasefire conditions and close a major loophole in international conflict.

items.title: Microsoft Hacked to Deliver Malware to Claude and Gemini Users
items.summary: Microsoft took the highly unusual step of shutting down more than 70 of its own GitHub repositories after hackers pushed malware that would steal credentials from AI coding agent users.

items.summary: CISA: Hackers now exploit SolarWinds Serv-U flaw to crash servers BleepingComputer

items.title: Exposed Fuel Tank Gauges Under Attack in the US
items.summary: Threat actors are taking advantage of Internet-exposed tank gauges by breaching gas stations, opening the door to disruption.

items.title: Soap Box: Detection and response in the AI age
items.summary: In this sponsored Soap Box edition of the Risky Business podcast Patrick Gray chats with Edward Wu, founder of Dropzone, about what AI is doing to detection, response and the SOC more generally. Dropzone makes AI agents that conduct alert investigations in your SOC, but will the SOC as we know it ev

items.title: Phishing Has Become an Industry, And AI Is Driving Its Growth
items.summary: By Matt Caffery, Senior Solutions Architect for Australia and New Zealand at Barracuda Networks For years, cybersecurity leaders have warned that email would remain the most effective pathway into organisations, [...]

items.title: China's TA4922 Expands Cybercrime Attacks Globally
items.summary: One of the world's most diverse, least-focused cybercrime groups is enlarging its footprint beyond East Asia.

items.title: Bugcrowd Launches EU Data Residency Option For Evolving Data Sovereignty Needs
items.summary: Organizations are growing serious about which nation's rules apply to their data. Experts point to geopolitical tensions as a main contributing factor.

items.title: Attackers Use AI to Automate EDR Evasion Testing
items.summary: Python scripts were used to test malware against endpoint detection and response agents from Sophos, CrowdStrike, and Windows Defender.

items.title: Cyber Insurance Rates Are Dropping, but Exclusions Widen
items.summary: Cyber insurance coverage is slowly changing, and some policies may not provide coverage for social engineering attacks like ClickFix.

items.title: Malicious Notifications Could Trick Google Gemini Users
items.summary: A prompt injection flaw in Google Gemini's voice assistant let attackers hide malicious commands in notifications, enabling social engineering and more.

items.title: Global Stock Exchange Hit by Monthslong Email Campaign
items.summary: A threat actor got a near-continuous view into an influential finance executive's email inbox, thanks to clever use of legitimate, native Windows tools.

items.title: Risky Business #840 -- Microsoft walks back researcher threats
items.summary: On this week’s show special guest co-host Andy Boyd joins Patrick Gray and James Wilson to discuss the week’s cybersecurity news. items.title: Over 116,000 Minecraft systems infected in WeedHack malware campaign - BleepingComputer
items.summary: Over 116,000 Minecraft systems infected in WeedHack malware campaign BleepingComputer

items.title: FBI-Flagged Phishing Kit Kali365 Expands Its Reach
items.summary: Once targeting just Microsoft 365, the phishing-as-a-service platform now aims at AWS, Okta, and Russian platforms, while relying on device code phishing.

items.title: DriveSurge Hijacks Thousands of Sites for ClickFix, FakeUpdate Attacks
items.summary: A sneaky, wide-scale IAB operation uses a malicious traffic distribution system (TDS) to redirect visitors of trusted websites to ones that deliver malware.

items.title: China Uses Dual-Method Cyberattack on Czech Orgs
items.summary: China is stealing data from high-value targets via a sneaky, double-layer spear-phishing campaign that includes the Azureveil malware.

items.title: Anthropic to Open Mythos AI to EU's ENISA
items.summary: The European security agency's entry to Project Glasswing is the result of "strong bilateral cooperation" between the European Commission and Anthropic.

items.title: Microsoft's Zero-Day Legal Threats Spark Backlash
items.summary: After a disgruntled security researcher published several zero-day exploits in recent weeks, Microsoft seemingly indicated criminal charges were in order.

items.title: WordPress malware campaign hides payloads in Steam profiles - BleepingComputer
items.summary: WordPress malware campaign hides payloads in Steam profiles BleepingComputer

items.title: Patch Now: Another Palo Alto Auth Bypass Bug Under Active Exploit
items.summary: Exploiting the PAN-OS GlobalProtect VPN vulnerability requires certain conditions, but adversaries have done so in two attack waves that started in mid-May.

items.title: 1,000 Data Breaches Later, the Disclosure Lag is Worse Than Ever
items.summary: Today, I loaded the 1,000th data breach into Have I Been Pwned. Reflecting on that milestone number, I pondered how to mark the occasion in writing, and what immediately came to mind was a very simple question: why is it still needed? Especially considering the emergence of privacy regulations

items.title: With Complex Cloud Integrations, Small Errors Lead to Major Compromises
items.summary: Researchers discover an exploit chain combining over-permissioned roles, secrets discovery, and non-human identities that could have compromised a popular automation service.

items.title: 'The Com' Cyberattacks Support Violence & Sexploitation
items.summary: Your organization's security failures have consequences for everyone else as well since this criminal gang uses its cyber winnings to support more violent and widespread crimes.

items.title: Dutch Raid Fails to Dent Russian Bulletproof Host
items.summary: Dutch law enforcement seized 800 servers and arrested two operators of THE.Hosting but left the hosting provider's core IP address space intact.

items.title: Focus on Cyber Insurance: How Quantifying Risk Is Reshaping Security
items.summary: In this latest installment of the Reporters' Notebook video series, we discuss how cyber insurance is forcing organizations to quantify risk, what's covered (and what's not), and why this could be the best thing to happen to cybersecurity.

items.title: BTMOB RAT Spreads Across Brazil, LatAm via MaaS Model
items.summary: An advanced remote access Trojan is propagating online. Notably, it's delivered via an operator licensing model and features a no-code malware-development interface.

items.title: Nordic CISOs Handle Rising Cyber Threats Remarkably Well
items.summary: Artificial intelligence notwithstanding, the vast majority of CISOs in northern Europe say they're facing no more serious cyberattacks than they did two years ago.

items.title: Australians more concerned about privacy as trust in AI remains low, OAIC survey finds
items.summary: A large majority of Australians say they are more concerned about privacy than they were five years ago, according to a new survey released by the Office of the Australian [...]

items.title: Latin American Cybercriminals Hoover Up Government Data
items.summary: A purported leak exposing 5.8 million records of Uruguayan citizens is the latest incident where cybercriminals targeted government agencies to monetize citizen data.

items.title: AI-Assisted Exploit Development Outpaces Scanner Detection
items.summary: Attackers are using AI to dramatically reduce the time they need to develop a working exploit for a CVE, according to new research.
